response.justification: Maria Rodriguez is a seasoned full-stack software engineer with over 15 years of experience in web, mobile, and some embedded systems development, but her profile is fundamentally misaligned with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, GPU architecture, and deep neural network workload analysis — none of which appear anywhere in her resume. Her closest relevant experience is low-latency application development at Dunnhumby and C/C++ component work at National Instruments, but these are software-level optimizations, not the hardware-software co-design or chip-level performance analysis the role requires.
strengths: 15+ years of broad engineering experience across multiple languages and platforms, demonstrating strong technical adaptability
Low-latency, high-throughput application work at Dunnhumby using async I/O, caching, and performance-oriented techniques in DLang
Exposure to C/C++ systems-level programming at National Instruments and some embedded/robotic systems work at Altom
concerns: Zero SoC or IP modeling experience — the foundational technical requirement of the role
No GPU architecture, AI accelerator, or deep-learning workload optimization background whatsoever
Entire career trajectory is software-focused (web, full-stack, DevOps); no evidence of hardware-software co-design or silicon-level performance analysis

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or GPU architecture experience
